front.title: IPv4 routes with an IPv6 next hop

abstract.t: V4-via-v6 routing is a technique that uses IPv6 next-hop addresses for routing IPv4 packets, and thus makes it possible to route IPv4 packets across a network where some routers have not been assigned IPv4 addresses. This document describes v4-via-v6 routing, and defines related operational procedures, notably the origination of ICMPv4 packets by nodes that might not have an IPv4 address.
